framing: Military veteran applying to cleared defense manufacturing role (Anduril-style JD)

jd.title: Manufacturing Operations Manager, Autonomous Systems

requirements.text: 7+ years experience in production-oriented or electro-mechanical technician fields with at least 3+ years in a supervisor or production operations lead/manager role in a high volume mixed product production environment
Complete understanding of product lifecycle from NPI to scaled production, LEAN manufacturing, and change management (ECOs)
Ability to interpret technical documentation including mechanical drawings, schematics, and equipment specifications
Process and equipment troubleshooting skills with adaptability to changing environments
Knowledge of 5S, lean manufacturing, or 6-Sigma principles
Currently possess and maintain active U.S. Secret security clearance
Associate's or Bachelor's degree in technical field (engineering, business, applied science)
Lean Six Sigma green belt certification
AS9100 standards and OSHA laws knowledge

responsibilities: Manage technician staff in production and hardware delivery through factory acceptance
Direct daily operations including work order issuance and completion
Ensure safety procedure compliance and maintain current safety training documentation
Coach, mentor, and support production technicians
Prioritize work to achieve on-time delivery targets
Provide weekly production progress reports, identify blockers and risks
Lead cross-functional daily communication with engineering, technicians, supply chain, logistics, and quality teams

expected_output.summary: Production operations leader with 10 years of U.S. Air Force experience managing electro-mechanical maintenance and manufacturing operations. Supervised 30+ technicians across avionics and weapons system production lines with active Secret clearance. Strong foundation in lean manufacturing, technical documentation interpretation, and cross-functional team leadership in high-tempo defense environments.

experience.company: U.S. Air Force — 309th Maintenance Wing, Hill AFB
experience.title: Production Supervisor, Avionics Repair
bullets: Supervised 30+ avionics repair technicians across 3 production cells performing depot-level maintenance on fighter aircraft systems
Managed daily work order flow using ABPDS (Air Force ERP system), tracking completion rates and backlog against delivery targets
Interpreted technical orders, schematics, and wiring diagrams for troubleshooting complex avionics failures
Led 5S implementation across repair facility, reducing tool search time by 40% and improving FOD compliance
Maintained 97% on-time delivery rate for critical avionics components supporting F-16 and F-35 fleet readiness

experience.company: U.S. Air Force — 56th Component Maintenance Squadron, Luke AFB
experience.title: Avionics Systems Technician / Shift Lead
bullets: Performed intermediate-level repair and testing of radar, EW, and navigation systems
Promoted to shift lead managing 12 technicians on swing shift operations
Trained 8 junior technicians on test equipment operation and technical order compliance
Supported NPI integration of upgraded radar warning receiver components into existing test workflows

experience.title: Avionics Systems Apprentice
bullets: Completed Air Force technical training in avionics systems maintenance (Sheppard AFB)
Qualified on 5 avionics test stations and earned 5-skill level certification

education: AS Applied Science, Aviation Maintenance Technology — Community College of the Air Force (2018)
Lean Six Sigma Yellow Belt — Air Force Continuous Process Improvement

jd_requirements: product lifecycle NPI scaled production lean ECOs
deliberate_flaws: Air Force ERP (ABPDS) not commercial ERP (NetSuite/SAP) — needs bridging
Yellow Belt only, not Green Belt
No AS9100 certification experience (military uses MIL-STD not AS9100)
No small electronic assembly (depot repair, not manufacturing)
AS degree from CCAF, not civilian institution — some employers undervalue this
Military jargon needs translation for civilian hiring managers
